top_signals.title: Stocks Fall as US-Iran Jitters Drive Oil to $100: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: A surge in oil spurred by the escalating Iran war sent stocks and bonds lower, with Wall Street also rattled by renewed concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will pay off.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear deal depends on relations with Israel
top_signals.summary: Crown prince has insisted any rapprochement with Israel would depend on steps towards establishing Palestinian state
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: ECB holds interest rates at 2.25% after debating rise
top_signals.summary: Some members of governing council ‘asked themselves whether we should not consider a hike’
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Tesla falls 10%, Alphabet sinks 5% as AI spending concerns spook investors
top_signals.summary: Both Tesla and Alphabet signaled higher spending as they invest in artificial intelligence.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Federal agencies broaden alert on Iran-linked OT attacks
top_signals.summary: The observed incidents include “malicious project file interactions and manipulation of data on human machine interface (HMI) and supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) displays,” the advisory says.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Thai firms pivot to defense as government pushes local procurement
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: LG to Ban Residential Proxies from Smart TV Apps
top_signals.summary: The home appliance giant LG Electronics USA said this week it plans to suspend any apps built for its smart TVs that turn one's television into an always-on residential proxy node. The move comes less than a month after researchers found that more than 42 percent of games and other apps available for download on LG's webOS store allow unknown third-parties to route their Internet traffic through a user's TV.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
